claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we got quite busy with ramping up shifts – adding shifts, getting people back in on the production lines.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool is ramping up production by adding shifts and bringing back employees to production lines.
claims.subject: Production ramp-up

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: midyear early third quarter, we should be starting to produce there.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool expects to start production at the new factory in West Virginia by midyear or early third quarter 2021.
claims.subject: West Virginia factory startup

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: double digit top line 10% to 12%, EBIT margin around 12%, and the investment level around €730 million.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool maintains its guidance for 2021 with a top line growth of 10% to 12%, EBIT margin around 12%, and investment level around €730 million.
claims.subject: FY2021 financial guidance

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: I definitely see that we should be able to grow 10% to 12%.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool expects to achieve a growth rate of 10% to 12% in 2021.
claims.subject: FY2021 revenue growth

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we could be relatively sure that we have 18 to 24 months of good business, good economic cycle.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool anticipates 18 to 24 months of favorable business conditions and economic cycle.
claims.subject: Market outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we will have further discussions on that. That combined with fuel transition over the next 10 years to meet our science-based target, that means we are probably more geared to staying on CapEx level that is not – you're not going to see €200 million from us.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool plans to maintain a capital expenditure level above €200 million to support growth and fuel transition over the next 10 years.
claims.subject: CapEx guidance

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: I think we will see a busy construction industry. I think we will see money going into renovation.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool expects a busy construction industry with increased investment in renovation.
claims.subject: Construction industry outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we should deliver the full-year guidance and you might see a quarter that is really, really good and some maybe a little bit lower or you don't see the end of those quarters.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool expects to deliver its full-year guidance despite potential quarterly fluctuations in performance.
claims.subject: FY2021 financial guidance

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we have the stimuli. We're all going to get vaccines. I feel comfortable that 2021 is a better year than 2019.
claims.paraphrase: Rockwool is confident that 2021 will outperform 2019 due to economic stimuli and vaccination progress.
claims.subject: 2021 performance outlook

claims.speaker: Mirella Vitale
claims.quote: the West Virginia facility has begun its running process in May, and we expect to be – begin all our commercial operations during the end of this month by late June.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects to begin all commercial operations at the West Virginia facility by late June 2021.
claims.subject: T-west-virginia-factory-startup

claims.speaker: Anthony Abbotts
claims.quote: we expect by the end of the year to be up around 15% or even more compared to the 35% that we need to achieve by end of 2022.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects to achieve around 15% energy efficiency renovation by the end of 2021, compared to the 35% target for the end of 2022.
claims.subject: T-2021-performance-outlook

claims.speaker: Anthony Abbotts
claims.quote: we've saved around €4 million of CapEx.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL has saved approximately €4 million in CapEx through the renovation of its office in Gladbach.
claims.subject: T-capex-guidance

claims.speaker: Anthony Abbotts
claims.quote: we've avoided 2,500 tonnes of construction waste going to landfill, and we've avoided going to landfill.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL has avoided 2,500 tonnes of construction waste going to landfill through its renovation project.
claims.subject: T-construction-industry-outlook

claims.speaker: Anthony Abbotts
claims.quote: we've avoided 1,800 tons of CO2 associated with the production of new construction materials if you had done a new build.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL has avoided 1,800 tons of CO2 emissions through its renovation project compared to a new build.
claims.subject: T-construction-industry-outlook

claims.speaker: Anthony Abbotts
claims.quote: we have been using our reclaimed waste service rock cycle during the renovation project.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL has utilized its reclaimed waste service, Rock Cycle, during the renovation project.
claims.subject: T-construction-industry-outlook

claims.speaker: Anthony Abbotts
claims.quote: we have achieved the DGNB gold certification, which is extremely difficult to attain for renovation.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL has achieved the DGNB gold certification for its renovation project, which is difficult to attain.
claims.subject: T-construction-industry-outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: I foresee that the beginning of the second half-year, pricing will improve gradually.
claims.paraphrase: Pricing is expected to improve gradually at the beginning of the second half of the year.
claims.subject: Pricing outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: And then, when we get to Q4, we're going to be in balance between inflation and price.
claims.paraphrase: By Q4, the company expects to achieve a balance between inflation and pricing.
claims.subject: Pricing outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: I would think that on the €370 million, the challenge you have when you go up on full capacity.
claims.paraphrase: CapEx is expected to be around €370 million, with challenges related to full capacity.
claims.subject: CapEx guidance

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We should start that line up end of first quarter 2022.
claims.paraphrase: The new line in Poland is expected to start up by the end of Q1 2022.
claims.subject: Poland line startup

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We are not moving as fast as we can to ramp up production. And so far, so good. But it comes at a good time.
claims.paraphrase: The ramp-up of production at the West Virginia factory is progressing, and it is timely given market conditions.
claims.subject: West Virginia factory startup

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: I think there is one important insight when you look at all of these directives, that is only the energy taxation directive that need a unified decision that everyone say yes.
claims.paraphrase: The energy taxation directive requires a unified decision for implementation, which may affect future business.
claims.subject: Market outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We are ahead of the plan.
claims.paraphrase: The company is ahead of its sustainability plan regarding CO2 emissions.
claims.subject: Sustainability progress

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We will do that.
claims.paraphrase: The company commits to upgrading the factory in Japan.
claims.subject: Japan factory upgrade

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We have started that and we have people coming on the ground now to start to plan that one out.
claims.paraphrase: The company has initiated planning for the factory upgrade in Japan.
claims.subject: Japan factory upgrade

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We are ramping up. We have certain stock, and there's no need to push sales in a certain segment if that – if you don't have the tonnes around then, that will be a delivery in December.
claims.paraphrase: The company is ramping up production and managing stock levels to optimize sales.
claims.subject: Production ramp-up

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We will get better after [indiscernible] (00:25:42).
claims.paraphrase: The company expects to improve energy efficiency in its buildings after completing ongoing projects.
claims.subject: Energy efficiency improvement

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We are happy with that, especially as the mix is a little bit skewed towards -- a bit more flat than we normally do.
claims.paraphrase: The company is satisfied with the EBIT margin despite a skewed product mix.
claims.subject: EBIT margin outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We are ramping up with bringing more ships, go higher up on the capacity utilization.
claims.paraphrase: The company is increasing capacity utilization to meet rising demand.
claims.subject: Capacity utilization

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We have a heavy winter, might decide to pause the project for a longer period and then wait for the material to arrive, and then we start off in the New Year.
claims.paraphrase: There is a risk that construction projects may pause due to material shortages and heavy winter conditions.
claims.subject: Construction market risk

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We have free capacity so we can grow next year.
claims.paraphrase: The company has free capacity to support growth in the next year.
claims.subject: Capacity for growth

claims.speaker: Kim Junge Andersen
claims.quote: We will not quantify specifically, but it's something that is between 0.5 and 1 percentage point.
claims.paraphrase: The impact of the Ranson factory startup on margins is estimated to be between 0.5 and 1 percentage point.
claims.subject: Impact of Ranson startup

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We are happy with that, especially as the mix is a little bit skewed towards -- a bit more flat than we normally do.
claims.paraphrase: The company is satisfied with the EBIT margin despite a skewed product mix.
claims.subject: EBIT margin outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We are ramping up with bringing more ships, go higher up on the capacity utilization.
claims.paraphrase: The company is increasing capacity utilization to meet rising demand.
claims.subject: Capacity utilization

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We have a heavy winter, might decide to pause the project for a longer period and then wait for the material to arrive, and then we start off in the New Year.
claims.paraphrase: There is a risk that construction projects may pause due to material shortages and heavy winter conditions.
claims.subject: Construction market risk

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We have free capacity so we can grow next year.
claims.paraphrase: The company has free capacity to support growth in the next year.
claims.subject: Capacity for growth

claims.speaker: Kim Junge Andersen
claims.quote: We will not quantify specifically, but it's something that is between 0.5 and 1 percentage point.
claims.paraphrase: The impact of the Ranson factory startup on margins is estimated to be between 0.5 and 1 percentage point.
claims.subject: Impact of Ranson startup

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we do not catch up on margin. This is now tighter above 13%
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects the EBIT margin to be above 13%.
claims.subject: FY2021 financial guidance

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: the CapEx forecast where we've taken it down a little bit. It doesn't mean that we have on purpose slowed down any capacity investments
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL has reduced its CapEx guidance to €320 million from €370 million due to a challenging environment.
claims.subject: CapEx guidance

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we are building inventory to make sure we have enough seasonal stock for the next high season
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL is building inventory to prepare for the next high season.
claims.subject: construction industry outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we will need a bit of time to normalize this, but it's progressing
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects to normalize gross margins over time, but it will take some time.
claims.subject: pricing outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we see that we're going to have Q4 where the gross margins are under – we haven't recovered
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL anticipates that gross margins will remain under pressure in Q4.
claims.subject: pricing outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we are certainly happy. I mean, we always like more margin but the Systems division also have this
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL is satisfied with a margin of 14% to 15% for the Systems division.
claims.subject: EBIT margin outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we are planning and executing on price increases so that we can get contribution margins and gross margins back to where we want them to be on a more normalized level
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL is planning to execute price increases to restore contribution and gross margins to normalized levels.
claims.subject: pricing outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: we have seen it so many time in Eastern Europe that sometimes it grows a lot, and then we have quarters when it doesn't grow as much
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL acknowledges that growth in Eastern Europe can fluctuate significantly from quarter to quarter.
claims.subject: construction market risk

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: the high growth areas continues to be North America and Eastern Europe
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects North America and Eastern Europe to remain high growth areas.
claims.subject: market outlook

claims.speaker: Kim Junge Andersen
claims.quote: the investment that we will do over the coming years in Russia for this factory and some other improvements we're making is – will be up to about €200 million
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL plans to invest up to €200 million in its Russian factory over the coming years.
claims.subject: Russia factory investment
